titleStmt.title: Letter from Gertrude Bannister to Colonel Maurice Moore, 22 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Gertrude Bannister

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Gertrude Bannister to Colonel Maurice Moore (1854-1939) in which she encloses clippings from The Times's of Sergeant O'Sullivan's defence of Roger Casement and suggests that they be reprinted in the Irish Press. The letter was written while Casement was imprisoned after he had been arrested while landing on the coast of Kerry in a submarine on the eve of the Easter Rising. Gertrude Bannister (d.1950) was a cousin of Sir Roger Casement (1864-1916). Bannister was heavily involved in raising public support for Casement during his trial for treason. Colonel Maurice George Moore was an Irish author, soldier and politician. Born in Co Mayo, he was the younger brother of the writer George Moore. He reached the rank of colonel in 1902. He resigned his commission in 1906 over matters of principle during the Boer War. In 1916 he he collected a petition with Agnes O'Farrelly asking for a reprieve of the death sentence against Roger Casement. Moore was later elected to the Senead Éireann in 1922.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
July 22 1916 at 36, Grosvenor Road, Westminster Dear Colonel Moore M rs. Green has asked me
to send you these cuttings from the
Times containing Serg t. Sullivan's defence
& R's statement. She thinks they should
be reproduced in the Irish Press â she
will probably explain the idea to you
more fully if she writes again â unless
she has already talked it over. If these
two things could be widely circulated
they might make an enormous difference.
No news yet about the Appeal to the
Lords. With Kind Regards
Yours sincerely Gertrude Bannister
